page: Amazon Web Services g6.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, delivering 16 virtual CPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM, and 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in specific workloads, particularly in large language model (LLM) inference. The instance achieves top-tier (top 10%) results for prompt processing and text generation using small (135M) and medium (7B) models, alongside strong (top 25%) prompt processing speeds for large (70B) models. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and text processing via Geekbench are also strong, placing in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, general memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range, while large-block memory write bandwidth is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g6.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads without requiring the higher-tier hardware profiles of larger GPU instances. The inclusion of a dedicated 22 GB L4 GPU and local NVMe storage makes it highly suitable for medium-scale LLM inference, graphics rendering, text processing, and general-purpose compute tasks that benefit from hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 600 GB local NVMe SSD. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, alongside strong single-core CPU and text processing capabilities. However, large-block memory write bandwidth remains a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25%. With a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps, this instance is well-suited for medium-scale AI inference, graphics rendering, and accelerated general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM for machine learning and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for AI inference and graphics.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and NVIDIA Ada Lovelace architecture for efficient machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 vCPUs via an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with an NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
Includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps
